name: Power
description: Calculates the electrical power consumed by the cell stack.

reference.title: Review of Capacitive Deionization
reference.description: Fundamental relation of power consumption in electrochemical systems.

name: Current Density
description: Electrical current per unit area of electrode.

reference.title: Electrochemical System Engineering
reference.description: Standard definitions for current density in porous electrodes.

example: I = 5 A, Area = 250 cm² => J = 0.02 A/cm²

name: Residence Time
description: Average time fluid stays inside the cell reactor channel.

reference.title: Chemical Reaction Engineering Principles
reference.description: Calculates hydraulic retention time for continuous reactors.

example: ReactorVolume = 2 L, FlowRate = 10 L/min => t = 0.2 min

name: Flow Velocity
description: Linear flow velocity through the spacer channel.

reference.title: Spacer Channel Hydraulics
reference.description: Flow modeling in thin spacer filled channels for CDI stacks.
reference.literatureReference: Modeling of flow structures in spacer filled channels

example: FlowRate = 10 L/min, ChannelArea = 1.1 cm² => v = 1.51 m/s

name: Hydraulic Diameter
description: Equivalent hydraulic diameter for rectangular flow channels.

reference.title: Fluid Mechanics for Rectangular Ducts
reference.description: Definition of hydraulic diameter for non-circular pipes.

example: Width = 100 mm, Height = 0.5 mm => Dh = 0.995 mm

name: Pressure Drop
description: Frictional pressure loss along the flow spacer channel length.

reference.title: Darcy-Weisbach Channel Friction
reference.description: Calculation of frictional pressure drop in spacer channels.
reference.literatureReference: Pressure loss in spacer-filled membrane modules

example: f=0.03, Length=200mm, Dh=1mm, Density=1000kg/m³, Velocity=0.15m/s => DeltaP=67.5 Pa

name: Pump Power
description: Theoretical electrical power required by the circulation pump.

reference.title: Pumping System Efficiency
reference.description: Standard energy requirements for fluid circulation in CDI setups.

example: FlowRate=10 L/min (1.66e-4 m³/s), DeltaP=180 Pa, PumpEfficiency=75% => PumpPower=0.04 W

name: Specific Energy Consumption
description: Energy spent per unit volume of desalinated water produced.

reference.title: Energy Consumption in Capacitive Deionization
reference.description: Metrics for comparing thermodynamic efficiency in CDI/EDI.

example: Power = 6 W, WaterProduced = 10 L/min (0.6 m³/h) => SEC = 0.01 kWh/m³

name: Salt Removal
description: Difference in total dissolved solids (TDS) between inlet feed and product outlet.

reference.title: TDS Profiles in CDI Desalination
reference.description: Basic mass transfer metrics for desalinating cells.

example: FeedTDS = 500 ppm, OutletTDS = 50 ppm => SaltRemoval = 450 ppm

name: Removal Efficiency
description: Percentage of salt removed relative to the feed salinity.

reference.title: Desalination Efficiency Metrics
reference.description: Percentage indicators for performance reporting in CDI.

name: Electrode Mass
description: Total mass of active electrode material in the cell.

reference.title: Carbon Electrode Engineering
reference.description: Determination of electrode mass from geometry and bulk material density.
reference.literatureReference: Porous carbon electrodes for CDI modules

example: Area = 250 cm², Thickness = 0.6 mm (0.06 cm), Density = 0.45 g/cm³ => Mass = 6.75 g

name: Salt Adsorption Capacity
description: Mass of salt adsorbed per unit mass of active electrode material.

reference.title: SAC in Capacitive Deionization
reference.description: Crucial optimization indicator representing charge storage capacity.
reference.literatureReference: Salt adsorption capacity of porous carbon electrodes

example: SaltRemoved = 135 mg, ElectrodeMass = 6.75 g => SAC = 20 mg/g

name: Charge Efficiency
description: Ratio of salt removed to electrical charge supplied during adsorption.

reference.title: Electrochemical Charge Efficiency in CDI
reference.description: Indicator of co-ion repulsion efficiency in electrical double layers.
reference.publication: Journal of Colloid and Interface Science
